strategies.intervention_thesis: Automation Assist could reduce revenue leakage in Claims processing without assuming unproven financial ROI.
strategies.why_this_fits: Automation Assist fits because the record shows revenue leakage, workflow redesign potential, and downstream impact in Cash collection.
strategies.expected_unlock: Relative unlock: improve Cash collection by reducing friction across Claims processing and Billing system.
required_evidence: Measure Claims processing using local operational logs.
Validate whether Preventable denial share materially changes the opportunity score.
key_assumptions: Operational data confirms recurring revenue leakage in Claims processing.
Staff can test changes in Claims processing without broad rollout.
Affected systems can produce before/after measurements.
strategies.first_experiment: Pilot automation assist on one bounded Claims processing workflow.
success_metrics: Reduced manual touches in Claims processing
failure_modes: Local records show little recurring revenue leakage friction.
Measured waste is materially lower than current assumptions.
Systems do not contain enough data to verify Claims processing.
strategies.recommended_next_step: Run a narrow pilot using: Classify denials by preventability before assignment
